rulings.Case Title: OAKLAND-ALAMEDA COUNTY COLISEUM AUTHORITY VS. GOLDEN STATE WARRIORS, LLC
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, April 10, 2019, Line 1 PETITIONER GOLDEN STATE WARRIORS, LLC PETITION TO VACATE ARBITRATION. Golden State Warriors, LLC's petition to vacate arbitration award is denied for the reasons set forth in the order granting the Authority's petition to confirm. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the Authority is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: MARC RITTENBERG VS. KENNETH KRAMARZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion Of Kenneth Kramarz And Tawonga Jewish Community Corporation Dba Camp Tawonga To Seal Records File In Support Of Motion To Enforce Settlement With Plaintiff Mark Rittenberg
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, April 10, 2019, Line 2, DEFENDANTS KENNETH KRAMARZ, TAWONGA JEWISH COMMUNITY CORPORATION'S Motion Of Kenneth Kramarz And Tawonga Jewish Community Corporation Dba Camp Tawonga To Seal Records File In Support Of Motion To Enforce Settlement With Plaintiff Mark Rittenberg. Defendants Kenneth Kramarz and Tawonga Jewish Community Corporation's motion to seal records is granted. No opposition filed and good cause shown per CRC 2.550 et seq. The Court finds that there exists an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the portions of the records to be sealed; the overriding interest supports sealing those records; a substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed; the proposed sealing is narrowly tailored; and no less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendants is required to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CRC 2.550(d) which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: MARC RITTENBERG VS. KENNETH KRAMARZ ET AL

rulings.Case Title: TAMMY VANNORSDALL VS. ESPETUS CHURRASCARIA INC. A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Court Approval Of Settlement And Dismissal Of The Paga Action
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, April 10, 2019, Line 5, PLAINTIFF TAMMY VANNORSDALL'S f Motion For Court Approval Of Settlement And Dismissal Of The Paga Action. Plaintiff Tammy Vannorsdall's motion for court approval of settlement and dismissal of PAGA action is granted. The Court approves the Settlement Agreement to the extent that it provides for the dismissal without prejudice of Plaintiff's PAGA causes of action, without any associated release of claims, in exchange for a mutual release of associated costs; thus, the Settlement Agreement will not prejudice the rights of other aggrieved employees. To the extent that the Settlement Agreement includes a release of Plaintiff's individual claims in exchange for other, unspecified consideration, court approval is not required. The Court will execute the proposed order included with the moving papers. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: DION MARTIN VS. AIRCRAFT SERVICE INTERNATIONAL INC. A CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendants To Produce Spreadsheet With Contact Information And Employee Information For Aggrieved Employees
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on the Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, April 10, 2019, line 2. PLAINTIFF DION MARTIN'S Motion To Compel Defendants To Produce Spreadsheet With Contact Information And Employee Information For Aggrieved Employees. Pro Tem Judge Peter Catalanotti, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff Dion Martin's unopposed Motion to Compel Defendants to Produce Spreadsheet with Contact Information and Employee Information of Aggrieved Employees is GRANTED. Defendants shall provide the document(s) within two weeks of notice of this order. Notice shall be provided by overnight mail. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to pcatalanotti@fmglaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES SALES VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, April 10, 2019, Line 6, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Motion To Quash Service Of Summons. Specially appearing Greg Wagner's motion to quash service of summons is granted. The service of summons on Mr. Wagner was improper because he is not a party to the action. (See Fuss v. City of Los Angeles (1958) 162 Cal.App.2d 643, 646 ["one 'who is not named either by his true or a fictitious name or as an unknown defendant is not a proper party to an action, and service of summons upon such person upon proper motion should be quashed.'"]). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for the City is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portions of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: PHILIP RASKIN VS. MARK LUTZ ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, April 10, 2019, Line 7, DEFENDANTS MARK LUTZ, NORTH BAY MPC LLC, NORTH BAY STEEL MILL SUPPLY & RECYCLING, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendants Mark Lutz, North Bay MPC, LLC ("MPC"), and North Bay Steel Mill Supply & Recycling, Inc.'s ("Bay Steel") demurrer is sustained and overruled in part. MPC's demurrer is overruled as to causes of action 1, 2, and 6. First, 28 alleges that MPC is a party to the contract and the court takes that allegation as true. Second, the court defers to plaintiff's interpretation of the contract on demurrer where plaintiff's position is not clearly erroneous. (See Rutherford Holdings, LLC v. Plaza Del Rey (2014) 223 Cal.App.4th 221, 229.) In this case, Lutz signed the operating agreement as MPC's manager and there is a factual issue regarding whether Lutz was acting as MPC's agent. Lastly, the Corporations Code indicates that a limited liability company is bound by and can enforce an operating agreement, which makes complete sense since the agreement controls how it functions. (See Corps. Code § 17701.11.) The demurrers are sustained with 20 days' leave to amend as to MPC as to causes of action 7 and 8. The gravamen of these claims is that Lutz misused his position to damage MPC's assets. That is a derivative claim and plaintiff should add MPC as a nominal defendant and make allegations regarding demand futility. (See Grosset v. Wenaas (2008) 42 Cal.4th 1100, 1108; Corps. Code § 17709.02(a)(2); PacLink Communications International, Inc. v. Sup. Crt. (2001) 90 Cal.App.4th 958, 964.) The demurrers are overruled as to Lutz and Bay Steel. The claims against Bay Steel regarding the failure to pay the 10% return and to provide financial documents and information are individual in nature. The demurrers by MPC as to causes of action 4 and 5 are sustained with 20 days' leave to amend. Plaintiff alleges that Lutz was self-dealing to MPC's detriment, e.g., causing MPC to incur improper debt. MPC correctly cites Casey v. U.S. Bank Nat. Ass'n (2005) 127 Cal.App.4th 1138, 1143-1144 for the proposition that absent alter ego allegations, wrongs of an individual acting adversely to a corporation are not imputed to the corporation. (See also 3 Witkin, Summary of Cal. Law (10th ed.2005) Agency and Employment, § 156, p. 200 ["Where an agent acts in a capacity adverse to the principal in the transaction, there is no reason to believe that the agent will keep the principal properly informed, and ordinarily the notice will not be imputed."].) The demurrers of Lutz and Bay Steel are overruled. The allegations that Lutz and Bay Steel colluded to misrepresent Plaintiff's 10 % return payments are actionable. Those allegations are not adverse as to Bay Steel. Lutz's demurrer to causes of action 4 and 5 based on the argument that the causes of action lack the requisite particularity are overruled. The first amended complaint sufficiently alleges the misrepresentations, and the charges are presumptively within Lutz's knowledge. The demurrer to the accounting cause of action is overruled. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)
